GPU-CPU particles parity: Support factor2 range randomization in gradients (#18283)

## Description

Upgrades the GPU particle gradient texture pipeline so that all
factor-based gradients (`addSizeGradient`, `addAngularSpeedGradient`,
`addVelocityGradient`, `addLimitVelocityGradient`, `addDragGradient`)
support the optional `factor2` parameter. When `factor2` is set, each
particle interpolates between `factor1` and `factor2` using its stable
per-particle `seed`, producing visible per-particle variation, matching
the CPU `ParticleSystem` behavior.

Previously, the GPU system ignored `factor2` entirely and baked only
`factor1` into a single-channel (R) texture, making all particles
uniform at each gradient stop.

## Changes

### Gradient helper consolidation (`baseParticleSystem.ts`)
- Moved `_addFactorGradient` (with sorting) and `_removeFactorGradient`
from both `GPUParticleSystem` and `ThinParticleSystem` into
`BaseParticleSystem` as shared protected methods, eliminating duplicate
logic

### Engine (`gpuParticleSystem.ts`)
- All five `addXxxGradient` methods now accept the optional `factor2`
parameter
- `_createFactorGradientTexture` produces **RG** (2-channel) float
textures instead of R (1-channel), storing `factor1` in the R channel
and `factor2 ?? factor1` in the G channel
- Simplified `_refreshFactorGradient` by removing redundant sort logic
(now handled by the base helper)
- Removed redundant `_releaseBuffers()` call from the old private
`_addFactorGradient`

### Shaders
- **GLSL** (`gpuUpdateParticles.vertex.fx`): All five gradient types
sample `.rg` and interpolate using per-particle `seed` components
- **WGSL** (`gpuUpdateParticles.compute.fx`): Matching changes for the
WebGPU compute path

### Seed component assignments
| Gradient Type | Seed Component |
|---|---|
| Size | `seed.y` |
| Angular Speed | `seed.z` |
| Velocity | `seed.w` |
| Drag | `seed.x` |
| Limit Velocity | `seed.y` |

### Visual Tests
Added 5 new factor2 range visual tests (CPU vs GPU side-by-side):

| Test | Snippet |
|---|---|
| GPU Particles - Change - Size Range | `#HWD2PR#0` |
| GPU Particles - Change - Speed Range | `#JPPZGY#0` |
| GPU Particles - Change - Speed Limit Range | `#UQ1RMS#0` |
| GPU Particles - Change - Angular Speed Range | `#MSX6FE#0` |
| GPU Particles - Change - Drag Range | `#Q1JW04#0` |

## Backward Compatibility

When `factor2` is omitted, both RG channels contain the same value, so
the shader interpolation is a no-op (`factor1 + (factor1 - factor1) *
seed = factor1`). Existing code that only passes `factor1` behaves
identically to before.

## Verification

- ✅ All 5 new factor2 range tests pass on WebGL2 and WebGPU
- ✅ All 12 "GPU Particles - Change" tests pass (no regressions)
- ✅ Lint passes
- ✅ Unit tests pass (2552/2552)

commit.message:
Add OpenAI-compatible backend; refactor VLMCoach to use backend abstraction

Architecture: 3 services
  1. Coral publisher (dev board): RTSP -> PoseNet@EdgeTPU -> ZeroMQ
  2. LLM endpoint: Gemini cloud OR local VM with OpenAI-compatible API
  3. Coaching server (laptop): ZeroMQ + rep counter + LLM scheduler + dashboard

vlm_coach.py:
- _Backend ABC with generate(table, window_seconds, frame_count, snapshot)
- _GeminiBackend: google-genai SDK, Vertex AI, response_schema enforcement
- _OpenAIBackend: openai SDK, configurable base_url/api_key/model
  - Tries json_schema response_format first (vLLM, LM Studio, OpenAI native)
  - Falls back to json_object + schema description in system prompt
  - Image sent as base64 data URI (works with multimodal local models)
- make_backend(settings) factory function; called from server.py
- VLMCoach.__init__ now takes backend=_Backend (not project/location directly)
- google/openai SDK imports deferred inside backend classes (import only what
  you use; avoids ImportError when the other SDK is not installed)

server.py:
- Reads LLM_BACKEND env var; builds _LLM_SETTINGS dict for the chosen backend
- Calls make_backend(_LLM_SETTINGS) and passes result to VLMCoach

requirements.txt: add openai>=1.0

.env.example:
- Renamed GEMINI_MODEL (was hardcoded MODEL constant)
- Added OpenAI block (commented out): OPENAI_BASE_URL, OPENAI_API_KEY, OPENAI_MODEL

commit.message:
feat(ci): rewrite GitHub Actions workflow for CI + tag releases

Replaces the placeholder desktop template with a working workflow:
- CI job: restore, build Debug+Release, run tests, upload .trx results
  triggers on push/PR to master
- Release job: triggered on v* tags only
  - publishes self-contained single-file win-x64 exe
  - injects version from the git tag (v1.2.3 -> 1.2.3)
  - zips output to FileSyncApp-<version>-win-x64.zip
  - creates GitHub Release with auto-generated notes and zip attached
  - marks as pre-release if tag contains a hyphen (e.g. v1.0.0-beta.1)

To release: git tag v1.0.0 && git push origin v1.0.0

* fix: use watch to trigger dashboard fetch when auth resolves in PortfolioView

Root cause: PortfolioView.onMounted fires before App.vue.onMounted's checkAuth()
completes (lazy-loaded components mount before their parent's onMounted). The
auth-gated fetchDashboard() was never called when authentication completed
asynchronously (after the component was already mounted).

Fix: Replace onMounted-only check with a watch({ immediate: true }) on
auth.isAuthenticated. This handles both:
  1. Auth already set when component mounts (loginAs flow)
  2. Auth set asynchronously after mount (checkAuth from App.vue onMounted)

The loading guard prevents double-fetches.

Also adds setAuthUser() helper to mock-api.ts for future use in tests that need
auth without the login form flow race condition.

commit.message:
refactor: remove CompiledRule<T> and CachedStringValidators

CompiledRule<T> wrapped Rule<T> with an OnceLock<CachedStringValidators>
for lazy caching, but CachedStringValidators was an empty struct — all
regex compilation already happens at Rule construction time via
CompiledPattern. The type added API surface and code with no runtime
benefit, and was never used in production code (only tests/benchmarks).

Removed:
- CompiledRule<T> struct, Clone/Debug impls, compile() methods
- CachedStringValidators empty struct
- All Validate/ValidateRef/Async trait impls for CompiledRule
- Re-exports from walrs_validation and walrs_inputfilter
- CompiledRule-specific tests and benchmarks
- Documentation references in README and design docs

CompiledPattern is unaffected — it remains independent.

refactor: strip GH_TOKEN from CLI, post reviews from orchestrator (#45)

Closes #40

## Summary

Refactors the token architecture so the Copilot CLI **never** receives a
GitHub API token. This is a prerequisite for adding `contents:write` to
the GitHub App — without this change, the review CLI would inherit push
access (prompt injection risk).

### Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `SKILL.md` | CLI writes review JSON to `.copilot-review.json` instead
of calling `gh api` |
| `copilot.py` | Removed `gh_token` param; explicitly pops `GH_TOKEN`
from env |
| `review.py` | Reads review JSON from worktree, posts via
`post_review()`, inlines stats |
| `github.py` | Added `post_review()`; removed dead
`append_stats_to_review` and `count_bot_reviews` |

### Token flow (before → after)

**Before:** CLI gets App token as `GH_TOKEN` → calls `gh api` to post
review
**After:** CLI gets NO GitHub token → writes JSON file → orchestrator
posts via App token

### Next steps (after merge)

1. Add `contents:write` to the homelab-review-bot GitHub App
2. Re-trigger #38 (Prometheus metrics) with `/implement`

* Map reqwest errors to semantically correct Twirp error codes

Replace the blanket From<reqwest::Error> → InvalidArgument mapping with
variant-specific logic:

- is_builder() → InvalidArgument (actually bad input)
- is_redirect() / is_body() / is_decode() → Internal (unexpected behavior)
- everything else (connect, timeout, request) → Unavailable (transient)

application errors, enabling correct retry decisions.

commit.message:
fix(ui): eliminate SplitterDistance crash on startup

Root causes:
1. WindowState=Maximized set in constructor triggers a form-level resize
   from the initial Size(1440,900) to actual screen size; the SplitContainer
   catches this transition mid-flight with invalid constraints
2. SizeChanged fires before WinForms has processed all WM_SIZE messages

Fix:
- Remove WindowState=Maximized (opens at 1400x860, user can maximise manually)
- Reduce Panel1/2MinSize from 320 to 100 (wider valid range, less risk)
- Set SplitterDistance via BeginInvoke in OnLoad — posts to the message queue
  and runs after every pending WM_SIZE/layout message, so Width is guaranteed
  non-zero and the 50% distance is always in-range

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve SSR hydration ordinal drift from structural blocks (#216) * fix: resolve SSR hydration ordinal drift from structural blocks and count element/text ordinals to map compiled template paths to SSR DOM nodes. When conditional () or repeat () blocks render content into the SSR DOM, the extra nodes shift ordinals so later siblings resolve to the wrong element. Root cause: the compiled template static HTML (meta.h) strips structural blocks, but the SSR DOM contains them inline between marker pairs (... and ...). The ordinal counting did not account for these extra ranges. This manifested in the commerce app when navigating from a zero-result search page (/search?q=test) to a category: the loop's parent container resolved to the

no-results message instead of

, leaving the product grid empty after SPA navigation. Framework changes (webui-framework): - Extract findByOrdinal() into markers.ts — walks parent children counting only nodes outside structural block marker pairs, with depth tracking for nested blocks of the same type. Both (element ordinals) and (text ordinals) now delegate to this single function. - Defer SSR marker removal — closing markers (, ) and item markers () are collected during hydration but only removed after all path-based resolution is complete (after for events/refs). This ensures marker pairs are intact whenever findByOrdinal needs to skip structural blocks, regardless of hydration phase ordering. - Add 10 unit tests for findByOrdinal covering: conditional skipping, repeat skipping, nested conditionals, multiple sequential blocks, empty blocks, text ordinal drift, interleaved block types, and edge cases (empty parent, out-of-range ordinal). Commerce app changes: - Fix mp-category-nav @attr mode — change all-active from string mode (default '') to boolean mode (default false). String mode produced '' (falsy) from the boolean attribute, causing a hydration mismatch where the server-rendered block was not tracked by the client, leaving orphaned 'All' DOM nodes visible after SPA navigation. - Fix Related Products scrollbar visibility — the scrollbar thumb used --webui-border (#262626), the same color as the track (--webui-surface #262626), making it invisible. Changed to --webui-text-muted and added scrollbar-width/scrollbar-color for Firefox cross-browser support. - Add regression test for search→category SPA navigation verifying category nav state and product grid population. - Update visual regression snapshots. Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> * Revert --------- Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: recursive array/object JSON Schema in tool params across all runtimes (#332)

* fix: recursive array/object JSON Schema in tool params across all runtimes

The wire-format conversion functions (_schema_to_wire, schemaToWire,
PropertiesToJsonSchema, parameters_to_json_schema) were producing flat
schemas like {"type": "array"} with no "items" for array properties and
no nested "properties" for object properties. This caused LLMs to
invent their own field names inside array items and objects.

The schema emitter types (ArrayProperty.items, ObjectProperty.properties)
were already correct — the bug was purely in the hand-written wire
conversion code that didn't recurse into nested types.

Fix: Replace flat schema conversion with recursive property-to-JSON-Schema
logic in all 5 runtimes:

- Python OpenAI/Azure/Foundry: _schema_to_wire delegates to _property_to_json_schema
- Python Anthropic: same pattern
- TypeScript OpenAI: schemaToWire delegates to propertyToJsonSchema
- TypeScript Anthropic: same pattern
- C#: new recursive PropertyToJsonSchema method in SchemaHelpers
- Rust OpenAI: new property_to_json_schema fn for tools + structured output
- Rust Anthropic: same for tools

Structured output (_property_to_json_schema / propertyToJsonSchema) was
already correct in Python and TypeScript. C# and Rust were also fixed
for structured output paths.

commit.message:
fix(ui): use SizeChanged one-shot handler to set SplitterDistance

OnShown fires before WM_SIZE delivers a real pixel width to the
Fill-docked SplitContainer, so _split.Width == 0 there too.

Replace with a SizeChanged lambda that:
- waits until Width > Panel1MinSize + Panel2MinSize + SplitterWidth
- sets SplitterDistance to Width / 2 (50/50 split)
- immediately unsubscribes itself so it only fires once

Also remove the unreliable OnShown assignment.
